dome_machine_readable_layer.assessment: Complete stasis at Day 28. The 2026-04-17 methodology burst (ai_manifest V51.1, claim taxonomy, review-response.html, api/ endpoint tree) remains the last substantive update on the dome's side. No new AI-steering infrastructure, no new evidence ladder entries, no registry changes.

index_html_meta.description: Independent scientific review of every prediction claimed by the Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model (ECM dome model). 69 claims evaluated — 0 survive scrutiny.
index_html_meta.og_description: 69 predictions claimed. 0 survive independent scrutiny. 18 acknowledged failures the model doesn't count.
index_html_meta.claim_review_schema: Accurate — references 69 dome WIN numbers, 94.5% accuracy figure, ratingValue 1 (False).

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day 28 of complete dome stasis. Their machine-readable layer froze April 17; ours is current and accurate. Both sites remain unindexed — a shared baseline condition, not a competitive gap. No new dome AI-steering infrastructure to assess. Our competitive position is stable: accurate files, current content, zero gaps that need closing this cycle.

human_decisions_needed.question: Bing Webmaster Tools verification
human_decisions_needed.context: GSC is confirmed DONE (google verification file present in docs/). Bing Webmaster Tools still pending — no BingSiteAuth.xml in docs/. Given neither site is indexed by any engine yet, this remains low priority. When indexing eventually happens, Bing coverage would be additive. Recommend completing when convenient.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 25-URL sitemap vs our 2-URL sitemap
no_action_gaps.reasoning: We have 2 pages (index.html + llms.txt). The dome has 25 because they have many sub-pages (wins.html, killshot.html, coordinates.html, live.html, audit.html, etc.). Our sitemap is complete relative to what actually exists. Adding phantom URLs would be dishonest.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has api/ endpoint tree (12 endpoints) with structured JSON
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Their API exposes registry, methodology, claim_index, etc. in machine-readable form. We could replicate this but it would be engineering overhead without indexing benefit currently. Worth revisiting once we're indexed. Not flagging as urgent.
